name: Unstuffed Cabbage
recipeIngredient: 6 Ginger snaps (for flavor) - or fresh or dried ginger to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine beef, water, eggs, 2 tsp dried minced onion, salt, and pepper. Mix thoroughly with your hands and form into golf ball-sized balls. Set aside.
2. In another large bowl, combine tomatoes, tomato paste, remaining dried minced onions, vinegar, and brown sugar to taste. Cut the tomatoes into chunks and stir into the sauce until well mixed. Add crumbled ginger snaps (or ground ginger) and stir. Taste and adjust flavor as needed; it should be sweet and sour. Set aside.
3. Core the cabbage and shred. Place half of the shredded cabbage at the bottom of a shallow casserole dish with a tight-fitting lid. Cover the cabbage with half the tomato mixture. Arrange the meatballs on top and cover with remaining cabbage. Top with the remaining tomato mixture. Cover tightly with foil and place the lid on top.
4. Bake at 325°F for one hour.
5. Reduce heat to 250°F and bake for two more hours.
6. The casserole may be cooled, wrapped, and frozen. For best taste, remove the casserole from the oven, let it cool overnight in the refrigerator. The next day, let the casserole come to room temperature and then bake uncovered at 300°F for 45 minutes. If eaten the same day, remove the lid for at least 45 minutes of the two-hour baking time. This recipe makes six to eight servings.
Note: You can stuff the cabbage leaves by blanching them first before stuffing and layering them, ensuring sauce is on the bottom and between each layer. Cook as described. The ginger snaps (or ground ginger) are what make the difference. Alternatively, you can brown the meat without making meatballs and then add the other ingredients. This recipe can also be made in a Crockpot.
